summary: In cases involving deep caries greater than 1mm or extensive decay, direct pulp capping should be avoided due to high risk of infection; instead, other treatments like pulpotomy are recommended based on current dental guidelines. Agent2 concluded after reviewing multiple sources that deeper cavities pose a significant risk when considering direct pulp capping as an option because they increase chances of microbial exposure leading to complications such as infections. Thus, appropriate treatment alternatives must be considered depending upon extent of tooth damage beyond what can safely managed through direct pulp cap procedure without risking patient health adversely. \boxed{\text{Direct pulp capping isn't suitable if caries exceed 1 mm}} This summary captures two critical aspects from the detailed analysis provided earlier while adhering strictly within specified length constraints given your request format requirements.
